proposals.statement: UniFirst's cost of revenues is the cost of physically running the rental service: as part of its rental business it picks up a customer's soiled uniforms and other items on a periodic basis (usually weekly) and delivers back cleaned and processed replacement items, so the cost line is dominated by in-plant processing (laundering) labor, route pick-up/delivery, and the merchandise it places in service.
proposals.quote: soiled uniforms and/or other items on a periodic basis (usually weekly) and deliver back cleaned and processed replacement items.
proposals.locator: Item 1 Business — Overview / Products and Services (p. 3-4)

proposals.statement: Energy and labor are core inputs to UniFirst's processing-and-delivery cost base: it discloses that inflation has at times pressured operating margins through increased energy costs for its vehicles and plants and increased wages in the labor markets in which it competes.
proposals.quote: increased energy costs for our vehicles and plants and increased wages in the labor markets in which we compete.
proposals.locator: Item 1A Risk Factors — inflation / energy and labor costs (p. 11)
